notes: Derived from NDC 2024 'Resetting Ghana' abridged manifesto text. Each row is a discrete editorial tracking unit; verify exact wording and page references against the party's canonical PDF before production comms.

promises.title: Scrap e-levy, COVID levy, 10% bet winnings tax, emissions levy, and selected import duties (industrial/agricultural equipment) within first 100 days
promises.description: Abridged manifesto: tax relief package to ease hardship and business costs.

promises.title: Apply Price Stabilisation and Recovery Levy on fuel to cushion consumers
promises.description: Fuel price stabilisation measure in economy reset chapter.

promises.title: Rationalise fees and charges at ports to reduce burden on importers
Comprehensive VAT reform (GETFund/NHIL decoupling, flat-rate reversal, higher registration threshold, repeal VAT on domestic electricity)
promises.description: VAT regime overhaul for households and MSMEs.

promises.title: Stabilise Ghana cedi and macro-economy through urgent recovery programme and stronger foreign reserves
Roll out equivalent of US$10 billion 'Big Push' for strategic infrastructure-led growth
'No-Fees-Stress' — no academic fees for first-year students in public tertiary institutions
Free tertiary education for persons with disabilities
Free primary healthcare (CHPS-led model as described in abridged text)
promises.description: Primary care access pledge; verify exact CHPS wording in PDF.

promises.title: Ghana Medical Care Trust ('MahamaCares')
promises.description: Dedicated medical care trust fund pledge.

promises.title: Free sanitary pads for schoolgirls (as listed among social interventions)
promises.description: Education/wash intersection; cited in economy social package list.

promises.title: Forensic audit into collapse of indigenous banks and financial institutions (cited GH¢25 billion cost in abridged text)
Restore licences of wrongfully collapsed financial institutions and support re-employment
Shift policy emphasis from over-concentration on macro-economy to micro-economy and real-sector investment
Economic Transformation Agenda (ETA) anchored on modernised agriculture, agribusiness and value addition
Reduce food inflation through Agriculture for Economic Transformation Agenda (AETA)
Revise Earmarked Funds Capping and Realignment Act 2017 to free GETFund, NHIL, Road Fund, etc.
Build economic buffers through external reserves to cushion the cedi
Review and enforce Fiscal Responsibility Act 2018 with Independent Fiscal Council and fiscal consolidation plan
Robust fiscal policy framework to reset priorities and cut waste and financial losses
Independent Value for Money office for high-value procurements (threshold set by Parliament)
promises.description: Procurement scrutiny; appears in economy and governance chapters.

promises.title: Constitute Independent Public Debt Management Office under PFM Act 2016 (Act 921)
Restore confidence on domestic bond and capital markets
Restructure Bank of Ghana to strengthen independence and operational efficiency
Forensic audit into alleged illegal currency printing financing prior government (hyperinflation impact cited)
Investigate Gold-for-Oil initiative and review Bank of Ghana Gold Purchase Programme
Improve disbursements into District Assemblies Common Fund (DACF)
Mo-Ne-Yo pension scheme for informal workers (drivers, okada, miners, farmers, fishers, traders, creatives, artisans)
24-hour economy — three eight-hour shifts to boost production, productivity and export-led growth
Women's Development Bank — low-interest tailored finance targeting ≥1 million women
National Apprenticeship Programme — free TVET, certification, start-up capital and equipment
'Adwumawura' — create, track and mentor ≥10,000 new businesses annually (youth focus)
Digital Jobs Initiative — US$3bn private-sector partnership (One Million Coders, regional digital centres, US$50m FinTech fund, zonal ICT parks)
Agriculture for Jobs — Feed Ghana Programme, farmer service centres in all districts, farm banks
Rapid industrialisation — agro-industrial zones by region, mini-processing plants, cocoa processing factories in listed regions, cashew factories Bono/Bono East/Ahafo, textile/cotton revival
Public sector employment reforms (as narrated under job-creation chapter)
promises.description: Editorial: align with exact public-sector bullets in PDF.

promises.title: National Employment Trust (as referenced in job creation narrative)
promises.description: Verify exact mandate wording in full/abridged PDF.

promises.title: Women's Development Bank — SME support package (tax incentives, financing, viable SME support)
promises.description: Cross-reference women's bank + SME enablers in 24-hour economy section.

promises.title: Exempt new small businesses from selected taxes for incubation period (as stated in SME section)
Tax breaks and incentives for strategic local investors (SME/industrial policy)
'Made-in-Ghana Agenda' for local production and procurement
'Export Ghana' programme to expand markets for Ghanaian goods and services
Review Customs procedures to facilitate legitimate trade
Leverage green enterprise incentives within 24-hour economy enablers
promises.description: Clean enterprise support; verify exact wording.

promises.title: Reduce bureaucracies and digitalise business-facing services
Promote technology-based businesses (AI, software, digital services)
Develop integrated aluminium industry to stimulate downstream jobs
Incentivise automotive and vehicle assembly expansion
Promote light manufacturing, especially apparel and garments
Support pharmaceutical industry to enhance local production of essential medicines
Promote modern technologies for plastics, ceramics, glass and allied industries
Revitalise strategic legacy industries (verify list in PDF)
promises.description: Editorial basket row — split after source review.

promises.title: 'Bright Beginnings Initiative' for early childhood education revival
'Furniture for All Initiative' with forestry commission partnership
Improve pupil-to-textbook ratio and aggressive school infrastructure programme
Improve Free SHS with dedicated window, decentralised school feeding procurement
Abolish double-track system for stable one-track academic calendar
Emergency infrastructure drive to complete abandoned/unfinished school projects
Continue free laptops/tablets for secondary students (policy continuation pledge)
Equip Science Resource Centres in all schools
Review Computerised School Selection and Placement System (CSSPS)
Establish regional TVET centres of excellence
Recognise Prior Learning Certification and National TVET Qualifications Framework pathways
Financial assistance for continuing tertiary students (loans, scholarships, allowances)
National health infrastructure plan — 500-bed specialist children's and fertility centre (Accra)
Refurbish Korle Bu Teaching Hospital as additional ultra-modern quaternary specialist hospital
Upgrade Greater Accra Regional Hospital (Ridge) to full teaching hospital linked to College of Physicians and Surgeons
Refurbish Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital to ultra-modern teaching standard
Expand Ho Teaching Hospital and establish Volta quaternary hospital for medical tourism
Specialist and trauma hospital in Oti Region (eastern corridor emergencies)
Modern dialysis centre in Upper East Region
Ultra-modern hospitals in Bawku, Yendi and other towns based on needs assessment
Upgrade Navrongo War Memorial Hospital for workforce training
Medical school in former Brong Ahafo region and operationalise Sunyani Regional Hospital as teaching site
College of Allied Health Sciences as postgraduate institute for allied health CPD
Complete additional UHAS campuses in Keta and Battor
Pharma hub strategy — make Ghana a pharmaceutical manufacturing hub in Africa
Feed Ghana Programme — SMART agriculture, farmer service centres nationwide, farm banks
Vegetable Development Project 'YƐREDUA' (greenhouses, irrigated open fields, urban/peri-urban support, home/school gardening)
Revamp poultry industry with incentives and 'Made-in-Ghana' local consumption push
'Poultry Farm to Table' project with poultry farmers associations
Livestock Development Project (cattle, piggery, small ruminants)
Feed Industry Programme for consistent local raw materials for agro-processing
Reintroduce free fertiliser, improved seedlings and inputs to cocoa farmers
'Youth in Cocoa Farming' — partnerships to revive abandoned farms
Cocoa farmer welfare package — price stabilisation fund, regular price increments, bonuses, health facilities, NHIS linkage, pension operationalisation
Produce at least 100,000 tons of cotton across five northern regions
New Strategic Crops Programme (cashew, palm, cotton, rubber) in agro-industrial zones
Agro-production enclaves with irrigation and networked warehousing
Refocus Exim Bank concessional support through cooperatives and community banks for export promotion
Reintroduce fertiliser subsidy programme to cut food inflation
National palm oil industry policy with incentives across value chain
Acquire land banks for commercial palm plantations and out-grower schemes
Invest in palm oil processing industries, research and development
Cap ministers at sixty (60) to reduce size of government
'Operation Recover All Loot' — investigate, prosecute and recover proceeds of corruption
Legislation banning political appointees, PEPs and serving officials from purchasing state assets
Forensic audit and inquiry into National Cathedral project (US$58m cited) and consensus on abandoned site
Inquiries into listed scandals (COVID-19 expenditure, SML, Agyapa, Math Sets, PDS, Sky Train, Pwalugu Dam, appearance fees, ambulance spare parts, illegal sole-sourcing, etc.)
promises.description: Bundled scandal inquiry row — split per editorial policy.

promises.title: Address Article 71 emoluments disparities vs broader public service
Scrap ex gratia payments
Establish Independent Emoluments Commission (merge Presidential Emoluments + Fair Wages bodies per constitutional review)
Special court for persons with adverse Auditor-General or Parliament findings
Repeal anti-press freedom laws; reverse intimidation; broaden media freedoms
Reopen investigations (2020 election killings, Ayawaso West Wuogon violence, murders of Ahmed Hussein-Suale and Silas Wulochamey)
New scholarship regime — prohibit scholarships to political appointees
Streamline scholarships across sectors to remove fragmentation
Align scholarships with national development priorities
Eliminate patronage, corruption, cronyism and nepotism in scholarship awards
Reserve government scholarships for vulnerable and marginalised on merit, equity and inclusion
Security programme aligned with 24-hour economy implementation
Purge militia and vigilante elements from security agencies
Clear backlog of promotions; merit-based transparent promotions
Merit-based decentralised recruitment and promotions across security services (HR gap analysis)
Enhance police visibility and neighbourhood patrols
Expand community-based crime prevention in high-crime areas
Enhance border security and counter terrorism financing cooperation
Prioritise welfare of serving and retired security personnel
New military training and recruit academy in northern sector
Nationwide community police programme
US$10 billion accelerated infrastructure plan — complete abandoned projects across sectors
Revamp Ghana Infrastructure Investment Fund (GIIF) for self-financing strategic projects
Water supply expansion (Ho/Sogakope/Hohoe, Tamale, Yendi/Sang, Savelugu/Pong-Tamale clusters as listed)
Address Bagre Dam spillage disaster sustainably
Develop Nasia, Nabogu and Pwalugu multi-purpose dams for all-year agriculture
Revamp Western Corridor rail for bulk cargo and passengers; extend corridors north
Reconstruct Eastern Corridor rail to Kumasi with northern freight/passenger extensions
Inner-city light rail for Kumasi and Takoradi linked to corridor lines
Urban inner-city light rail for Accra
New railway Sekondi-Takoradi through Western North, Ashanti, Ahafo, Bono, Bono East, Savannah to Hamile
Major road programme — Golden Triangle A-3T highway and orbital motorways (Accra, Kumasi, Takoradi, Tamale, Sunyani, Techiman)
Eastern, Western and Upper road corridors and inter-regional roads programme (as listed in manifesto)
promises.description: Bundled feeder/trunk roads; split after verification.

promises.title: 'Light Up Ghana' — resume universal street lighting programme for 24-hour economy
Resolve upstream oil and gas disputes with government promptly
Premix fuel management policy with landing beach committees for equitable distribution
Apply ESLA revenues for their intended purpose
Energy transition strategy — nuclear plus solar, wind, biogas, waste-to-energy, mini-hydro (Juale, Pwalugu, Pra cited)
Foreign policy chapter — protect inviolability of diplomatic premises per Vienna Conventions
Streamline dual citizenship and diaspora democratic participation
Diaspora investment platforms and expertise contribution programmes
Negotiate e-visa arrangements to facilitate travel and trade
